def: v.n. To be finished, &c. (see ćuk-jānā, the com. form); — ćuk-jānā, v.n. To be finished, be completed; to be exhausted (as a supply or store), to come to an end, to cease ( = ho-ćuknā); to be settled, be fixed, be agreed upon (as a price, &c.); to be settled, be adjusted, be adjudicated (as a difference or dispute, or a law-suit); — to fall short (of), to fail (see ćūknā)
subs: adj. (f. -ī) & s m. Falling short (of), failing; — one who fails (to do anything).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀘𑀺𑀓𑁆𑀓𑀡 (cikkaṇa) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit चिक्कण (cikkaṇa).

ety: ćūk˚ = Prk. चुक्क(इ), fr. S. च्युत्+कृ; cf. ćuknā
def: v.n. To mistake, to blunder, to commit a fault or error; to depart (from), to deviate (from), to go astray, to wander, to err, to miscarry; to be neglectful or deficient (in), to fall short (of), to fail; to fail to hit (a mark), to miss; to be short (of); to be missing; to be inadvertently omitted
s.m. Sourness; acid seasoning; vinegar made by the acetous fermentation of grain; Indian sorrel, Rumex vesicarius.
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit चक्र (cakra). Doublet of چرخہ (carxa), چاک (cāk), چکر (cakr), and چَکَّہ (cakka).

def: s.f. A fractional division of land.
s.f. Wood-sorrel, Oxalis monodelpha, or Rumex vesicarius.
s.m. A handful ( = bukṭā).
adj. (f. -ī), Settled, fixed; definite, conclusive; — wholesale.

s.f. A pinch, &c. ( = ćuṭkī, q.v.). s.f. A pinch; a handful; snapping the fingers; a knot in cloth (made by a dyer to prevent a part taking the dye); hammer (of a gun); a twist given to lace; stretching cloth after it is sewn; a portion of grain (about two seers) given to a weighman as his fee; a medicinal powder; a ring worn (by women) on any of the toes (except the great toe)
subs: adv. In the time it takes to snap the fingers, in a moment, in a trice
v.n. To produce a snapping sound by means of the thumb and middle finger, to fillip with the fingers (as an accompaniment to a song, &c.)
As much as can be held between a finger and the thumb, a pinch (as of snuff, &c.)
or bhar-lenā, ćuṭkī toṛnā (-kī or -meṅ) = ćuṭkī lenā, q.v.; — ćuṭkī ćaṛhānā (-par), To test the soundness of coin by putting a pinch of testing powder on it ( = ćuṭkī lagānā)
v.t. To nip, to pinch, &c. ( = ćuṭkī lenā)
(-meṅ), To apply the fingers (to), to pick (a pocket); to test coin ( = ćuṭkī ćaṛhānā, q.v.); to fold up (a leaf, &c.); to tear (cloth, &c.) with the tips of the fingers

(-kī or -meṅ), To pinch, nip; to give a fillip (to), to stir up, to kindle; to throw out provoking insinuations or innuendoes; to utter cutting words or sarcasm; to wound or hurt (the feelings, e.g. dil-meṅ ćuṭkī lenā)
v.t. To turn off in a joke; to turn into ridicule.

def: s.f. Settlement; agreement; decree or sentence (of a court, &c.).

ety: S. चुड्डा, rt. चुड्ड or चुल्ला, rt. चुल्ल्; see ćulćulānā
def: s.f. Longing, craving, prurience, itching, itchiness (fig. & lit.), titillation; the itch ( = ḵẖārish); eagerness, impatience, restlessness, uneasiness, fidgetiness; wantonness, lust, sexual passion
subs: To satisfy a longing or craving (for); to satisfy carnal desire or lust.

def: s.f. A tenon, the part of a joiner's work which fits into another, a dove-tail; the foot of a door or the pivot on which it turns in a socket in the threshold; an axle-tree arm
subs: ćūleṅ ḍhīlī honā or ho-jānā, v.n. 'The joints to become dislocated, or to become loose'; to be tried, to be worn out or exhausted (with labour, &c.).
wety: Borrowed from Chagatai چول (çöl).

def: s.m. = ćūlhā, q.v. v.t. To cast into the fire
subs: s.m. An invitation to a feast given to one member of every household.

def: s.f. A crest, &c. ( = ćūḍā, ćūṛā, q.v.). s.f. = ćūṛā, q.v. s.m. Powder; filings; sawdust; bruised grain (to be chucked into the mouth); a small piece, a fragment
subs: v.t. To break to atoms; to reduce to powder; to bruise, to pulverize

ety: ćulak˚ = Prk. चुलक्के(इ), fr. S. चुल्ल (or चुड्ड)+कृ
def: v.n. To itch, to feel or suffer titillation.
v.t. To cause to drip, to distil, &c. ( = ćuwānā, q.v.). v.t. To cause to drop or drip, to distil, filter, draw off, drain; — to direct (speech) at, to talk at.
wety: From Sanskrit चिल्ल (cilla) (kite (bird)) + آ (ā, causative augment) + ـنا (-nā, verbal suffix).

def: s.m. A kind of dish prepared of boiled rice (without meat; cf. pulāʼo).
s.f. A crest ( = ćūlā, ćūḍā).
s.f. Restlessness; playfulness, &c. ( = ćulbulāpan, q.v.). s.m. Restlessness, fidgetiness; sportiveness, liveliness, gamesomeness, gaiety; wantonness, coquettishness.
adj. (f. -ī), Restless, fidgety; airy, gay, sportive, lively; wanton, coquettish.
s.f. = ćulbulāpan, q.v. s.m. Restlessness, fidgetiness; sportiveness, liveliness, gamesomeness, gaiety; wantonness, coquettishness.
v.n. To be restless or fidgety, to fidget, to be agog; to roll or toss about (in sleep, fever, &c.).

ety: ćulbulā+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. Restlessness, fidgetiness; sportiveness, liveliness, gamesomeness, gaiety; wantonness, coquettishness.
adj. = ćulbulā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Restless, fidgety; airy, gay, sportive, lively; wanton, coquettish.

ety: v.n. of ćulćulānā; by redupl. of ćul, q.v.
def: s.m. Restlessness; prurience; &c. ( = ćul); wantonness, inconstancy.

ety: ćulćulā˚ = Prk. चुलुचुल(इ); cf. S. चुड्ड् and चुल्ल्
def: v.n. To suffer from prurience, or itching; to itch, to titillate; to feel lust, feel the sexual passion.
v.n. (dialec.) To drop, drip, trickle ( = ćūnā).
s.f. = ćul-ćul, q.v. s.m. Restlessness; prurience; &c. ( = ćul); wantonness, inconstancy.
v.t. To cast into the fire
subs: s.m. An invitation to a feast given to one member of every household.

def: s.f. A lewd or libidinous woman.

ety: S. चुड्डा or चुल्ल+कार+कः; cf. ćul
def: adj. (f. -ī), Wanton, lustful, lascivious, lewd.
adj. (f. -ī), = ćulhārā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Wanton, lustful, lascivious, lewd.
v.t. To cast into the fire
subs: s.m. An invitation to a feast given to one member of every household.

def: s.f. = ćullī; ćūlhī; and ćolī, qq.v. adj. & s.m. Lewd, &c. ( = ćulhārā); — a libidinous man; — a catamite.
wety: Borrowed from English Chile, from Spanish Chile.

def: s.f. Itching, titillation, &c. (cf. ćul-ćul).
adj. (f. -ī), Blear-eyed; purblind ( = ćundhlā).
adj. & s.m. Blear-eyed; — a blear eye.
adj. & s.m. Lewd, &c. ( = ćulhārā); — a libidinous man; — a catamite.
wety: Borrowed from English Chile, from Spanish Chile.

def: s.f. A fire-place; a chimney ( = ćūlhī); — the support placed beneath a stack of straw or store of grain, staddles.
wety: Borrowed from English Chile, from Spanish Chile.

def: s.m. The palm of the hand hollowed so as to hold water, &c.; a mouthful (of water); a handful (of any liquid)
subs: s.m. As much (water, &c.) as can be contained in the hollowed palm of the hand, a handful (of any liquid)
lit. 'To drown oneself in a handful of water'; to be greatly or unreasonably abashed
To sip; — to acquire a habit of drinking by gradual increase; to become gradually rich
v.t. To drink (water, &c.) out of the hollowed palm
(-kā), To rinse the mouth before and after eating, &c.
To become as stupid as an owl (or to become intoxicated) with a mouthful.

def: v.t. (prov.) = ćūrnā, q.v. v.n. To be stolen ( = ćorī honā).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀘𑀮𑀤𑀺 (caladi), from Sanskrit चलति (calati), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *čálHati, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *čálHati, from Proto-Indo-European *kʷél-e-ti, from *kʷel- (“to move; to turn (around)”). Compare Persian چلیدن (čalīdan, “to go; to walk”), Persian چالاک (čâlâk, “fast, agile, clever”).

def: s.m. (dialec.) = ćūlhā, q.v. v.t. To cast into the fire
subs: s.m. An invitation to a feast given to one member of every household.

def: s.m. A kiss
subs: (-kā), To take a kiss, to kiss.

def: s.m. = ćumbak, q.v.
subs: s.m. = ćumbak-patthar. s.m. A loadstone, a magnet; — an extract (from a book, &c.); — a quotient; a general scholar, one who knows parts of a variety of books; — the upper part or middle of a balance; — a rogue, cheat
s.m. 'The kissing stone'; a loadstone ( = ćumbak).

def: s.m. Kissing.
v.t. To cause to kiss; — to trifle with, play with, to disappoint.
s.m. The ceremony of kissing the crown of the head, and (by women) the feet also.
s.m. A kiss( = ćūmā, q.v.). s.m. A kiss
subs: (-kā), To take a kiss, to kiss.

def: s.m. A loadstone, a magnet; — an extract (from a book, &c.); — a quotient; a general scholar, one who knows parts of a variety of books; — the upper part or middle of a balance; — a rogue, cheat
subs: s.m. 'The kissing stone'; a loadstone ( = ćumbak).

def: s.m. = چمبك ćumbak, q.v. s.m. A loadstone, a magnet; — an extract (from a book, &c.); — a quotient; a general scholar, one who knows parts of a variety of books; — the upper part or middle of a balance; — a rogue, cheat
subs: s.m. 'The kissing stone'; a loadstone ( = ćumbak).

def: s.m. = ćummal, q.v. s.m. = H چملي चुमली ćumlī, s.f. (dialec.) A ring (of cloth, grass, &c.), to place under a load upon the head, or under a pitcher or vessel ( = ćuṅbal; syn. jūnā).
s.m. Kissing; a kiss (cf. ćūmā).
s.m.(f.?), A kissing sound made with the lips to coax, or quiet, or to call children, horses, dogs, &c.; cheeping (syn. ćućkār).
wety: From چَمَک (camak, “bright; glitter”) + ـار (-ār).

def: s.f. = ćumkār, q.v.
subs: (-ko) = ćumkārnā. s.m.(f.?), A kissing sound made with the lips to coax, or quiet, or to call children, horses, dogs, &c.; cheeping (syn. ćućkār).

def: v.t. To make a kissing sound with the lips in order to coax or quiet, &c.; to chirp or cheep to, to coax, to soothe, to cheer; to speak kindly to; to fondle.
s.f. = H چمل चुम्मल ćummal, s.m. (dialec.) A ring (of cloth, grass, &c.), to place under a load upon the head, or under a pitcher or vessel ( = ćuṅbal; syn. jūnā).
s.m. A kiss ( = ćūmā, q.v.)
subs: (-kā), To take a kiss, to kiss.

def: s.m. = ćumbak, q.v. s.m. A loadstone, a magnet; — an extract (from a book, &c.); — a quotient; a general scholar, one who knows parts of a variety of books; — the upper part or middle of a balance; — a rogue, cheat
subs: s.m. 'The kissing stone'; a loadstone ( = ćumbak).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀘𑀫𑀓𑁆𑀓 (camakka), perhaps from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀘𑀫𑀓𑁆𑀓 (*camakka).

def: s.m. = H چملي चुमली ćumlī, s.f. (dialec.) A ring (of cloth, grass, &c.), to place under a load upon the head, or under a pitcher or vessel ( = ćuṅbal; syn. jūnā).
v.t. (prov.) = ćūmnā, q.v.

ety: Prk. चुंवणअं; S. चुम्बनीयं, rt. चुम्ब्
def: v.t. To kiss; to lick; to fawn upon
subs: To make much of for a time and then cast aside, to weary of a thing or object much desired before possession
(or ćūm-ćām-ke) khānā, To lick (the platter) clean
adj. Fit to be kissed; inviting a kiss; loveable, lovely.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀘𑀼𑀁𑀩𑀤𑀺 (cuṃbadi), from Sanskrit चुम्बति (cumbati, “kisses”), from चुम्ब् (cumb), ultimately from Dravidian. Compare Tamil சூம்பு (cūmpu, “to suck, fondle with the lips”), Kumarbhag Paharia [script needed] (cumqe, “to kiss”).

def: s.f. A slight or low noise or sound; squeaking, a squeak; creaking; the sound of breaking wind
subs: ćūṅ-ćūṅ, ćūṅ-ćahāṭ, s.f. Creaking; squeaking; chirruping, chirrup (of birds, grasshoppers, &c.); twittering, twitter; warbling, song, voice
s.m. Making a suppressed sound or noise; the sound of breaking wind
To make not the slightest noise, to remain perfectly quiet.

def: s.m. Powder; flour, meal; pulse coarsely ground ( = ćūnī); lime ( = ćūnā)
subs: adj. (f. -ī), 'Of powder or dust'; frail, weak.

def: adv. Like, in the manner of, as, such as; when; — how? why? wherefore?; — conj. Because, forasmuch as, &c. ( = ćūṅ-ki)
subs: adj. & s.m. Without likeness, incomparable; — the incomparable One, the Deity
ćūṅ-o-ćirā, Why and wherefore?; wrangling, altercation, dispute
ćūṅ karnā, To demand why and wherefore; to question, to object; to wrangle, dispute
To ask no questions, to remain silent
adv. & conj. When that; seeing that, whereas, forasmuch as, inasmuch as, since.

def: s.m. Lime, slaked lime (vulg. called 'chunam'); white powder or dust
subs: (meṅ), To put a coating of lime (on); — to defeat; to defame
v.n. To become powder, to turn to dust (as bones)
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀘𑀼𑀡𑁆𑀡 (cuṇṇa), from Sanskrit चूर्ण (cūrṇa, “lime; powder”).

ety: ćū˚ = Prk. चुअ(इ) or चोअ(इ) = S. च्योत(ति), rt. च्युंत् (or श्र्च्युत्)
def: v.n. To drop (as ripe fruit from a tree, &c.); to drop, leak, distil, ooze, exude; to be filtered; — to secrete; — to menstruate.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀘𑀼𑀡𑁆𑀡 (cuṇṇa), from Sanskrit चूर्ण (cūrṇa, “lime; powder”).

def: s.m. = ćūnā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Picked, gathered, culled; selected, arranged, laid (as dishes); plaited
